title: Public shipped an MCP server for a live brokerage - full options trading, real accounts, one pip install
author: BuildwithPublic
selftext:
Been building on the r/PublicApp trading API for months- today the MCP was open-sourced. 

**What it does:**  
Connects Claude (or any MCP client) to a live brokerage account. Not a paper trading sim. Real orders.

**Tools exposed:**

* `get_quotes` — real-time stocks, options, crypto
* `get_option_chain` — full chain with strikes, bids, asks
* `get_option_greeks` — delta, gamma, theta, vega, rho, IV
* `place_order` — single leg (market, limit, stop)
* `place_multileg_order` — spreads, straddles, iron condors
* `preflight_multileg_order` — dry-run cost estimate before placing
* `get_portfolio`, `get_orders`, `get_history`

**Install:**

    pip install publicdotcom-mcp-server
    

Add to `claude_desktop_config.json`, drop in your API key, done. Claude now has a brokerage.

**What I've been doing with the underlying API:**  
Built an automated earnings scanner that scores options setups, SPX SPY 0DTE systems. mean-reverting equities bots, GEX IV fits, fully autonomous. The MCP server makes all of that accessible through a conversation instead of code. Unlimited degrees of freedom. 

Repo: [github.com/PublicDotCom/publicdotcom-mcp-server](http://github.com/PublicDotCom/publicdotcom-mcp-server)  
Signup: [public.com/signup](http://public.com/signup)

Happy to chat on the implementation - been very deep in this API stack for months.

comments.author: dogazine4570
comments.body:
This is pretty wild — exposing live brokerage actions through MCP is a big step beyond the usual “LLM + paper trading” demos.

A few questions I’d love clarification on:

- How are you handling auth/session security? Is this OAuth with scoped tokens, and can users enforce read-only vs trade permissions?
- Any built-in safeguards on `place_order` / `place_multileg_order` (e.g., max position size, confirmation hooks, rate limits)?
- For options, are Greeks calculated server-side or pulled directly from the brokerage feed?
- How are you thinking about latency for real-time quotes + execution? Especially for multi-leg spreads where fill quality matters.

Also curious whether there’s any audit logging layer so users can review exactly what the model requested vs what was executed.

Very cool direction overall — but with real money involved, guardrails and observability are everything. Would love to see a quick architecture overview if you’ve got one.

replies.author: BuildwithPublic
replies.body:
Fantastic questions I see a lot -in practice here's how it works:

**Auth/security:** API keys are generated through your Public account settings. They're scoped to your account and you control them -  can revoke anytime. The skill itself doesn't store credentials persistently. Within Claude, you paste your key into the session and it's used for that workflow only.

**Safeguards on order placement:** Every order runs through a preflight endpoint before execution -iit returns est cost, buying power impact, fees, and margin reqs. The skill won't skip that step. On top of that, the risk layer I built in Claude enforces position limits, daily loss limits, and circuit breakers before any order hits the API. You can configure those thresholds yourself. Nothing executes without passing preflight first.

**Greeks:** Pulled directly from the API via the `get_option_greeks` endpoint - not calculated client-side. So you're getting the Public broker side values, not a local Black-Scholes approximation.

**Latency:** Honest answer here-  this isn't built for HFT or latency-sensitive scalping. Quotes come from the market data endpoints, execution goes through REST. For multi-leg spreads on earnings plays or hedges where you're thinking in minutes not nano/milliseconds, it works well. If you need sub-10ms fills you'd want a different stack.

**Audit logging:** Every order placed through the API is logged in your account history with full detail - what was requested, what filled, timestamps, prices. The skill can also fire alerts on every fill and every stop hit so you have a real-time record outside the platform.

The whole point is closing the loop between research and execution without pretending it's something it's not. It's a decision-making and execution layer at core. 

What architecture are you looking for?
